channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A fifth of US butterflies have disappeared since 2000: Study
item.description: Butterflies are disappearing from the United States at an alarming rate, with their total population declining by more than a fifth over two decades, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job” and close the Education Department. McMahon was confirmed by the Senate on Monday, and an executive order to shutter the department could come as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis records audio greeting from hospital to thank people for their prayers
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis recorded an audio greeting Thursday to thank people for their prayers as he continued his recovery from double pneumonia in stable condition with no new respiratory crises or fever, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump slashed teacher training, citing DEI. Educators say the grants fought staff shortages
item.description: Trump administration cuts to teacher training grants are putting a strain on rural school systems, which have relied on the money to help address teacher shortages.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: Pope Francis remains so stable that Vatican says next update will be provided Saturday
item.description: Given the stability of Francis' condition, doctors said they didn’t expect to provide a new medical update until Saturday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger doesn't want Amazon shopping list revealed at trial: court filings
item.description: Bryan Kohberger is asking a Boise judge to exclude his Amazon shopping list and 'click activity' from the University of Idaho student murders trial in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KTLA 5

item.title: Louisiana and Arkansas look to nitrogen executions
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Hours after the nation’s first execution by nitrogen gas in January last year, Alabama's attorney general urged other states to also develop it as a method for carrying out death sentences. Now, some states are following suit.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, KIM CHANDLER and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has dropped a high-profile abortion case in Idaho. Here's what that means
item.description: A yearslong legal battle over the right to an emergency abortion in Idaho has been abruptly upended now that President Donald Trump has moved to drop the high-profile case.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi And Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is unwilling to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs on the United States if President Donald Trump leaves any U.S. tariffs on Canada, a senior government official told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 3 skiers buried in Alaska avalanche, officials say
item.description: An avalanche in the Alaska backcountry swept away and buried three skiers who were believed to be dead under a pile of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: By MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ launches investigation into UC system over antisemitism allegations
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department announced on Wednesday that it has launched a civil investigation into the University of California system under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act. Under Title VII, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AbbVie enters into almost $2B contract with Gubra for obsesity drug
item.description: Bloomberg reports the obesity drug market is expected to reach as much as $130 billion in annual sales by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican introduces censure resolution against Al Green
item.description: The House will vote this week on a resolution from Rep. Dan Newhouse (R-Wash.) to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his outburst at President Trump’s speech to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Mysterious affliction causing endangered Florida fish to spin and die is back
item.description: Following a roughly nine-month lull, there have been 22 reports of the odd spinning behavior involving sawfish in waters around the Florida Keys since December.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California congressmember pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KTLA 5

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jon Stewart accepts Elon Musk's demands for 'Daily Show' interview, fires back at his criticism
item.description: Jon Stewart is rejecting Elon Musk's criticism that he's a "propagandist," and added that he's "game" for an interview with the tech billionaire if he agrees to appear as a guest on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans target 4 'sanctuary' cities as Trump pushes mass deportations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress are taking aim at four cities — often called “sanctuary cities” — over their policies limiting cooperation with immigration enforcement with a hearing this week that comes as President Donald Trump presses ahead with his campaign of mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: Companies that depend on cross-border trade were already facing higher prices in anticipation of the 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada that took effect Tuesday. Now disruption looms, and if consumers won’t accept steep price hikes, they’ll have to downsize operations and lay off workers.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
